perturbation.original_text: Buyer will not contact the Seller or Seller's employees, customers, suppliers or agents other than Broker for any reason whatsoever without the prior written consent of the Broker. All contacts with the Seller or such other parties will be made through or by Broker unless otherwise agreed to by Broker, in writing. Seller’s employees may not be aware of the sale of the Business and Buyer agrees not to disclose to Seller’s employees that the Business is for sale, nor identify that Buyer is a possible purchaser of the Business.
perturbation.changed_text: Buyer will not contact the Seller or Seller's employees without the prior written consent of the Broker. Buyer is encouraged to contact the Seller's customers, suppliers or agents as needed. All contacts with the Seller or such other parties will be made directly by the Buyer. Seller’s employees may be aware of the sale of the Business and Buyer may disclose to Seller’s employees that the Business is for sale, and identify that Buyer is a possible purchaser of the Business.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the original clause regarding contact with the Seller's network. Originally, all contact required the Broker's consent. The modification encourages direct contact with customers, suppliers, and agents. Furthermore, it states that sellers employees may be aware and that the buyer can openly contact and disclose who they are and that the business is for sale, completely contradicting the intentions and need for confidentiality outlined in the first part of this same section and in other sections of the document.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Buyer will not contact the Seller or Seller's employees without the prior written consent of the Broker. Buyer is encouraged to contact the Seller's customers, suppliers or agents as needed. All contacts with the Seller or such other parties will be made directly by the Buyer. Seller’s employees may be aware of the sale of the Business and Buyer may disclose to Seller’s employees that the Business is for sale, and identify that Buyer is a possible purchaser of the Business. 4. The Information furnished by Broker has been prepared by or is based upon representations of the Seller and Broker has made no independent investigation or verification of said Information. Broker makes no representation that the Information is accurate or complete. Buyer hereby expressly releases and discharges Broker from any and all responsibility and/or liability in connection with the accuracy, completeness or any other aspects of the information and accepts sole and final responsibility for the evaluation of the Information and all other factors relating to the Business. Buyer understands and acknowledges that it has been advised to engage attorneys, accountants and other advisors to complete due diligence on the Business offered for sale to verify that the Business is satisfactory. Buyer understands that the future success or failure of the Business is primarily dependent on the Buyer’s knowledge and efforts and is not dependent on the Business’s past volume of business or profitability.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text strictly prohibits contact with the Seller's employees, customers, suppliers or agents without the Broker's consent. The changed text encourages contact with customers, suppliers, and agents, and allows disclosure of the sale to employees. This directly conflicts with the contradicted text, creating a strong in-text contradiction as defined by 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Broker makes no representation that the Information is accurate or complete. Buyer hereby expressly releases and discharges Broker from any and all responsibility and/or liability in connection with the accuracy, completeness or any other aspects of the information and accepts sole and final responsibility for the evaluation of the Information and all other factors relating to the Business.
perturbation.changed_text: Broker guarantees that the Information is accurate and complete. Broker will be held responsible and/or liable in connection with the accuracy, completeness or any other aspects of the information and Buyer is not responsible for the evaluation of the Information and all other factors relating to the Business.
perturbation.explanation: This revision directly contradicts the original liability and responsibility allocation. Initially, the Broker disclaims accuracy and liability. The change now makes the Broker liable for accuracy, which creates an in-text contradiction. The buyer's sole responsibility of evaluation is also changed where the buyer is no longer responsible.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Information furnished by Broker has been prepared by or is based upon representations of the Seller and Broker has made no independent investigation or verification of said Information. Broker guarantees that the Information is accurate and complete. Broker will be held responsible and/or liable in connection with the accuracy, completeness or any other aspects of the information and Buyer is not responsible for the evaluation of the Information and all other factors relating to the Business. Buyer understands and acknowledges that it has been advised to engage attorneys, accountants and other advisors to complete due diligence on the Business offered for sale to verify that the Business is satisfactory. Buyer understands that the future success or failure of the Business is primarily dependent on the Buyer’s knowledge and efforts and is not dependent on the Business’s past volume of business or profitability.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original allocation of responsibility and liability. The original text states the Broker makes no representation of accuracy and is released from liability, while the changed text states the Broker guarantees accuracy and will be held liable. This is a direct reversal of terms. The contradicted text mirrors the changed text and is in contradiction with the original text.

perturbation.original_text: The Information is subject to change or withdrawal without notice and the Business is being offered for sale subject to prior sale or the withdrawal of said offering without notice.
perturbation.changed_text: The Information cannot be changed or withdrawn, with notice, and the Business is not being offered for sale subject to prior sale or the withdrawal of said offering without notice.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation creates uncertainty regarding the availability of the information and business. The original clause states the information can change without notice. The changed clause asserts the information is fixed, contradicting the ability of brokers to add/remove information or retract the business for sale.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Information cannot be changed or withdrawn, with notice, and the Business is not being offered for sale subject to prior sale or the withdrawal of said offering without notice. 6. Buyer will indemnify and hold harmless the Broker and Seller from any and all claims or actions arising from Buyer's acts or failures to act in pursuing the possible purchase of the Business, including, without limitation, reasonable attorney's fees and other expenses incurred by Broker and seller. 2 7. Buyer will not, for a period of three (3) years from the date hereof, enter into any agreement for the purchase of the Business, in whole or in part, or assist or promote any other party in so doing, unless such agreement to purchase provides for commission to be paid Broker, with the commission being defined as the amount agreed upon by Broker and Seller in the "Business Listing Contract”, “Engagement Agreement” or similar agreement between those parties. If Buyer violates the foregoing provision, Buyer will be liable for and pay any commission that would have been due to Broker by Seller upon demand without any obligation on Broker's part to first exhaust any legal remedies against Seller. 8. Buyer represents that Buyer has sufficient financial resources to complete the transaction for the asking price and terms set forth herein. Buyer agrees to provide, upon request by Broker or Seller, financial statements, credit references and other pertinent information evidencing such financial sufficiency. 9. The performance and construction of this Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the Commonwealth of Pennsylvania. All sums due hereunder shall be payable at the office of the Broker in Bucks County, Pennsylvania. The parties agree to the exclusive jurisdiction of the state and federal courts in and for Bucks County, Pennsylvania to enforce the terms of this Agreement. Claims for injunctive relief may be filed in any court with appropriate jurisdiction over the matter. All other claims must be arbitrated in accordance with section 11 below. 10. This Agreement shall be binding upon the Buyer, Buyer's heirs, executors, successors, assigns, administrators or representatives. If any provision of this Agreement shall be held to be invalid, void or unenforceable, the remainder of the provisions hereof shall remain in full force and effect and this Agreement shall be construed as if such invalid, void or unenforceable provision had not been contained herein.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the Information cannot be changed or withdrawn, with notice, and the Business is not being offered for sale subject to prior sale or the withdrawal of said offering without notice. The contradicted text repeats the changed text. The explanation given states that the original clause allowed the information to change without notice and the business to be withdrawn. The changed clause, mirrored in the contradicted text, asserts the information and business are fixed. This is a structural flaw because the information may actually be withdrawn, but the repeated clause in the contract now states otherwise. This causes uncertainty and a direct conflict, aligning with the Structural Flaws definition and creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Buyer will not, for a period of three (3) years from the date hereof, enter into any agreement for the purchase of the Business, in whole or in part, or assist or promote any other party in so doing, unless such agreement to purchase provides for commission to be paid Broker, with the commission being defined as the amount agreed upon by Broker and Seller in the "Business Listing Contract”, “Engagement Agreement” or similar agreement between those parties. If Buyer violates the foregoing provision, Buyer will be liable for and pay any commission that would have been due to Broker by Seller upon demand without any obligation on Broker's part to first exhaust any legal remedies against Seller.
perturbation.changed_text: Buyer will, for a period of ten (10) years from the date hereof, enter into any agreement for the purchase of the Business, in whole or in part, or assist or promote any other party in so doing, even if such agreement to purchase does not provide for commission to be paid Broker. If Buyer violates the foregoing provision, Buyer will not be liable for or pay any commission that would have been due to Broker by Seller upon demand without any obligation on Broker's part to first exhaust any legal remedies against Seller.
perturbation.explanation: This changes the terms regarding the duration and commission obligation. The original clause prevents the buyer from engaging in a purchase for 3 years without ensuring the broker's commission. Now the duration is 10 years, and the buyer is now obligated to enter into a purchasing agreement, and even if the broker is not paid a commission the buyer won't be held liable. This contradicts the purpose of the agreement where the broker's commission is meant to be secured.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Buyer will, for a period of ten (10) years from the date hereof, enter into any agreement for the purchase of the Business, in whole or in part, or assist or promote any other party in so doing, even if such agreement to purchase does not provide for commission to be paid Broker. If Buyer violates the foregoing provision, Buyer will not be liable for or pay any commission that would have been due to Broker by Seller upon demand without any obligation on Broker's part to first exhaust any legal remedies against Seller. 8. Buyer represents that Buyer has sufficient financial resources to complete the transaction for the asking price and terms set forth herein. Buyer agrees to provide, upon request by Broker or Seller, financial statements, credit references and other pertinent information evidencing such financial sufficiency. 9. The performance and construction of this Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the Commonwealth of Pennsylvania. All sums due hereunder shall be payable at the office of the Broker in Bucks County, Pennsylvania. The parties agree to the exclusive jurisdiction of the state and federal courts in and for Bucks County, Pennsylvania to enforce the terms of this Agreement. Claims for injunctive relief may be filed in any court with appropriate jurisdiction over the matter. All other claims must be arbitrated in accordance with section 11 below. 10. This Agreement shall be binding upon the Buyer, Buyer's heirs, executors, successors, assigns, administrators or representatives. If any provision of this Agreement shall be held to be invalid, void or unenforceable, the remainder of the provisions hereof shall remain in full force and effect and this Agreement shall be construed as if such invalid, void or unenforceable provision had not been contained herein.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the original clause such that the buyer is now permitted to enter into agreements for the business purchase without ensuring the broker's commission, and the buyer won't be liable even if the broker isn't paid. The contradicted text restates this changed clause. The perturbation explanation details that this change directly contradicts the original intent of securing the broker's commission. Since the changed and contradicted text combined eliminates the broker's commission guarantee while the original agreement sought to ensure it, this is a direct contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Any controversy between the parties arising out of the terms, obligations, covenants or conditions of this Agreement, shall be arbitrated in Bucks County, Pennsylvania on written request of one (1) party served on the other. Such arbitration shall be under the rules of the American Arbitration Association. The arbitrator shall have no authority to change any provisions of this agreement; the arbitrator’s sole authority shall be to interpret or apply the provisions of this Agreement. The expenses of arbitration conducted pursuant to this paragraph shall be born by the parties in such proportion as the Arbitrator shall decide.
perturbation.changed_text: Any controversy between the parties arising out of the terms, obligations, covenants or conditions of this Agreement, shall be litigated in Philadelphia County, Pennsylvania. Such litigation shall be under the rules of the American Litigation Association. The arbitrator shall have full authority to change any provisions of this agreement; the arbitrator’s sole authority shall be to re-write the provisions of this Agreement. The expenses of litigation conducted pursuant to this paragraph shall be born solely by the Broker.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation entirely alters the dispute resolution process, creating a contradiction. Originally, disputes were to be arbitrated in Bucks County with a limited scope for the arbitrator. The modified clause mandates litigation in Philadelphia County, grants the arbitrator full power to rewrite provisions, and makes the broker solely responsible for all expenses. This shifts the power away from the arbitration to litigation that the buyer is guaranteed to win as the Broker will pay for all expenses.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Any controversy between the parties arising out of the terms, obligations, covenants or conditions of this Agreement, shall be litigated in Philadelphia County, Pennsylvania. Such litigation shall be under the rules of the American Litigation Association. The arbitrator shall have full authority to change any provisions of this agreement; the arbitrator’s sole authority shall be to re-write the provisions of this Agreement. The expenses of litigation conducted pursuant to this paragraph shall be born solely by the Broker. 12. The terms and conditions of this Agreement shall also apply to any other business and/or property on which Broker has been retained to represent the owner(s) in the sale thereof and on which Broker or owner(s) has furnished information to Buyer. Further, it shall not be necessary for Buyer to execute any additional agreement(s) to that effect and any terms and conditions of this Agreement that refer to the date hereof shall automatically be adjusted to reflect the date on which Broker or owner(s) initially furnished information to Buyer on such other business and/or property. 13. The provisions hereof cannot be modified, amended, supplemented or rescinded without the written consent of Broker and this Agreement sets forth the entire agreement and understanding of the parties hereto. EXECUTED ON THIS ______ DAY OF ___________________, 201___. ____________________________________ ________________________________ Typed/Printed Name of Buyer Typed/Printed Name of Buyer (additional buyer/partner) ____________________________________ ________________________________ Signature (Individually and as Signature (Individually and as Duly Authorized Representative) Duly Authorized Representative) ____________________________________ ________________________________ Street Address E-mail Address ____________________________________ ________________________________ Telephone (fax) ____________________________________ ________________________________ Telephone (voice) Telephone (mobile) Stony Hill Business Brokers, LLC
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the dispute resolution process by moving it from arbitration in Bucks County to litigation in Philadelphia County, changing the rules from American Arbitration Association to American Litigation Association, granting the arbitrator (or rather, the court) full authority to change provisions of the agreement, and placing the financial burden of litigation solely on the Broker. This contradicts the original intent and creates a direct conflict. The contradicted text restates this altered dispute resolution process, but the core contradiction lies in the shift from arbitration to litigation and the changed financial responsibility, making the Broker liable for all litigation expenses, which is a significant and direct contradiction of standard business practice and the original intent of arbitration, which is generally a shared expense. Clause 13 states the provisions hereof cannot be modified, amended, supplemented or rescinded without the written consent of Broker, which this change does without consent.
